pr.title: lots of clean up in player, remove ifdefs, and remove a bunch of decomp links, rename labels, etc
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Large cleanup of src/melee/pl/player.c focused on replacing NON_MATCHING/asm fallbacks with C versions that were already matching or intended to be used, removing many embedded decomp.me links and commented-out symbol notes, and renaming several raw data labels to more descriptive names. The PR substantially reduced file noise: one changed file with 385 additions and 7697 deletions. Evidence is from the title and diff excerpt; there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Removed the local `#define NON_MATCHING 1` gate from `player.c`.

Deleted many `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` / `#else asm ... #endif` blocks and kept the C implementations for player functions such as `Player_GetPtrForSlot`, `Player_80031790`, `Player_80031848`, `Player_80031900`, `Player_800319C4`, and `Player_80031AD0` as shown in the excerpt.
Renamed data symbols from raw labels to descriptive names: `lbl_803BCDC0` became `str_PdPmdat_start_of_data`, `lbl_803BCDCC` became `str_plLoadCommonData`, and `lbl_803BCDE0` became `ftMapping_list`.
Updated comments and references to use the new `ftMapping_list` name, including accesses in `Player_80031AD0`.
Changed the helper `checkNegOne(s8* num)` into `hasExtraFighterID(ftMapping* data)`, making the helper express the semantic test on `extra_internal_id != -1`.
Removed or shortened many decomp.me scratch links and commented-out extern/string/sdata notes that had been kept as temporary decomp scaffolding.
Kept a TODO around an unnamed BSS struct near `lbl_804587E0`, indicating the author did not have enough evidence to fully name that object yet.
smart_moves: Converted raw label-based data names into searchable semantic names only where the data meaning was clear from contents and usage, especially the fighter mapping table and common-data strings.
Reduced maintenance burden by deleting thousands of lines of inline assembly fallback once C versions existed, making future player work easier to read and modify.
Centralized the repeated slot bounds assertion pattern behind `Player_CheckSlot`, then used direct `player_slots[slot]` access in C helpers.
Improved type intent by making the extra-fighter check operate on `ftMapping*` instead of an arbitrary `s8*`.
Preserved uncertainty with TODOs rather than over-naming ambiguous data such as the BSS allocation object around `lbl_804587E0`.
Updated comments alongside symbol renames so future searches for `ftMapping_list` would find both declaration and use sites.
decomp_lessons: Once a function's C implementation is trusted or matching, remove the `NON_MATCHING` conditional and inline asm fallback to avoid stale duplicate sources of truth.

Prefer semantic names for data tables and strings after their role is clear: table contents and access patterns can justify names like `ftMapping_list`, while raw labels should remain only when evidence is weak.
Helpers should encode domain meaning, not just byte-level behavior: `hasExtraFighterID` is more useful than `checkNegOne` for later struct cleanup.

When struct layout is still imperfect, use comments to document the mismatch rather than forcing an inaccurate abstraction; the excerpt notes a more sensible `ftMapping_list` pointer expression was off by one byte.
Large asm deletions are safest when paired with small, readable C equivalents that preserve the same slot checks, loop bounds, and function calls seen in the disassembly.
Cleanups that remove decomp.me scratch links should leave enough local semantic context so future contributors do not need external scratch pages to understand the code.
naming_conventions: Use descriptive string names with `str_` prefix for known string data, e.g. `str_PdPmdat_start_of_data` and `str_plLoadCommonData`.
Use domain/table names for arrays formerly named by addresses, e.g. `ftMapping_list` instead of `lbl_803BCDE0`.
Use predicate-style helper names for boolean checks, e.g. `hasExtraFighterID` for testing `extra_internal_id != -1`.
Keep generated/address labels such as `lbl_804587E0` when the object meaning is not yet established, optionally with a TODO documenting uncertainty.
Player functions in this file retain the existing `Player_800xxxxx` naming pattern where no semantic function name is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed inline PowerPC asm blocks guarded by `#else` after `#ifdef NON_MATCHING`, leaving C functions in place.
Used C loops over fixed player slot counts, notably slot range `0..5` and per-player loop bounds of `2` in functions that call `func_800867E8` or `func_8008688C`.
Preserved the original slot validation behavior through `Player_CheckSlot`, which reports `cant get player struct! %d\n` and asserts on invalid slots according to the excerpted asm/comment context.
Accessed `player_slots` as an array of `StaticPlayer` rather than manually reproducing the assembly's `slot * 0xe90` address arithmetic.

For the `ftMapping` extra ID byte, used a byte-offset workaround: `offset_arr = (s8*) (&ftMapping_list[0].extra_internal_id)` and indexed by `player->player_character * sizeof(ftMapping)`, with a comment that the more natural table pointer expression was off by one byte.
Matched table-driven fighter behavior by reading `internal_id`, `extra_internal_id`, and `has_transformation` from `ftMapping_list`.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that continue renaming `Player_800xxxxx` functions to semantic names.
Search for remaining references to `lbl_803BCDE0`, `lbl_803BCDC0`, or `lbl_803BCDCC` after this cleanup to confirm the rename propagated fully.
Investigate whether the TODO near `lbl_804587E0` was later resolved into a named allocation/global struct.
Search for uses of `hasExtraFighterID` to see whether the TODO about using it in more places was completed.
Review later player.c PRs for further removal of decomp.me scratch links and raw label comments.
